---
title: "Graph querying"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

The two key functions are `getNodes` and `getDependencies`/`getParents`.

- `getDependencies` (`getParents`) determines children (parents) by traversing the `downstreamRules` (`upstreamRules`) using `traverseGraph`.
   - input is `varRange` (or `nodeRange`).
   - output is `varRange`.
   - if nodes are needed, pass output through `getNodes`

- `getNodes` generates relevant `nodeRanges` by applying the various `nodeRule` subclasses (`declRule`, `rhsRule`, `topRule`, etc.)
   - allows one to select stoch/determ, top/latent/end, RHSonly.
   - optionally can take `varRange` or equivalent (`nodeRange`, variable name, string representing `varRange`) so that operates only on part of the model.
